name: CORTICAL CONTUSION
definition: CEREBRAL CONTUSION = BRAIN CONTUSION traumatic injury to cortical surface of brain

demographics.Incidence: most common type of primary intraaxial lesion; in 21% of head trauma patients;
demographics.Path: petechial hemorrhage (= admixture of blood with native tissue) followed by liquefaction

dominant_finding: "salt and pepper lesion" = mottled / speckled densities as focal / multiple (29%) poorly
discriminating_features: "salt and pepper lesion" = mottled / speckled densities as focal / multiple (29%) poorly
diffuse cerebral hypodensity + swelling without hemorrhage in immediate posttraumatic
some degree of contrast enhancement ← leaking new capillaries
true extent of lesions becomes more evident with progression of edema + cell necrosis +

general: "salt and pepper lesion" = mottled / speckled densities as focal / multiple (29%) poorly
diffuse cerebral hypodensity + swelling without hemorrhage in immediate posttraumatic
some degree of contrast enhancement ← leaking new capillaries
true extent of lesions becomes more evident with progression of edema + cell necrosis +
hemorrhagic lesion (detected in 50% of all contusions):
initially decreased intensity (← deoxyhemoglobin of acute hemorrhage) surrounded by
hyperintense on T1WI + T2WI in subacute phase ← Met-Hb
hyperintense gliosis + hypointense hemosiderin on T2WI in chronic phase
nonhemorrhagic lesion hypointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI
